rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: If any vendor limits were ambiguous, include a short mapping table in the memo showing the exact packet text line(s) referenced for each encoded rule to remove any residual ambiguity.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include a short table in the memo showing the baseline and both +5% and -5% recomputed priorities per record (R1..R4) to make sensitivity evidence immediately verifiable without opening the workbook.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Memo documents application of +/-5% perturbations to normalized metrics and an 'expert_review_flag' that is set when any record classification changes; current state reports no class changes (flag = FALSE). Portfolio and classification outputs do not indicate missed triggers. Sensitivity procedure and flagging are present and tied to workbook logic per the memo.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: For completeness, include an explicit budget-check line in the final decision table showing chosen combo cost sums and the budget comparison to make verification trivial.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Cost table in memo and portfolio_options in actual_outputs show costs 18600, 28200, 32400 and combined scenarios; O2+O3 is correctly flagged infeasible at 60600 > 60000 budget. The final decision states no mitigation selected with justification that priorities remain below hold threshold and budget checks were performed.

This matches the rubric requirement to flag any record that changes classification under the 5% test. Deduction is small due to lack of executable verification of the workbook scenarios.
